I visited Los Tres Amigos located in Jackson MI last week and the restarant was gorgeous.  The food also was tasty and well prepared.

I did encounter a serious problem with the manager.  He brought our food to our table for our waitress who was very polite and wonderful server, with excellent people skills.  The manager gave me "Ricaro Morales as his name.  He was rude and uncourteous.  After eating I talked with him and he remained unapologetic.  I filmed our conversation.

I would be happy to talk to someone in management more about this.

I haven't been here since they redecorated, and it was shocking walking in to this disaster of decoration. The theme is so terribly overdone. There is no need to have every possible surface plastered with bright, distracting "Mexican" art. The food is standard tex-mex, not bad, not amazing.

Fantastic service. Heard the service was quick, and that was no joke. Time from placing our order to receiving the food was 6 minutes! Not only that, but it was very clear that the majority of the food (obviously not rice and beans) was not prepared. High quality food, good price, and quick service: best Mexican restaurant I've ever been to.

The manager flat out asked if we were paying or not, while the girl working the register stated "well they used it they have to pay". review_text:
I like this restaurant for many reasons but sometimes service is lacking. It's your standard Tex-Mex cuisine, not authentic Mexican at all. Fajitas, queso amarillo, burritos are all Tex-Mex creations. Not saying they're not delicious, but it's important to be honest about the type of cuisine you're serving and most of the food on the menu would be found in Texas and not México.
Prices are fair for the portions you receive.
Would recommend this restaurant to others craving Tex-Mex. If you want authentic Mexican, try Toñitos on Ganson near East St.

We thought they were served warm???? So I asked and he said "Come back tomorrow" and hopefully they will warm.!?!? If I want a Chicken Quesadila , I will make a better one at home......for 1.50 vs the 6.50 I was chardged. In the end, Service =2.75 Stars. Food =2.5 Stars.  Hospitality/Genuine Concern 2 Stars ! Value = 2 Stars . I know what restaurants are capable of and those who meet and/or exceed Guests expectations. Los Tres barely almost met average: in todays "just get by" short term restaurant thinking. 2.5 Stars. Just ordered a "Molcajete" and a "Big Burrito Amigos" through DoorDash and I am extremely disappointed in the quality of the food. The Molcajete is a few random cuts of meat tossed into a flavorless sauce. I thought paying a premium $14 so dollars for a meal would be worth it. Absolutely not. I ate a quarter of it and thew it away.
The Big Burrito Amigos was alright. The three small tortillas they include to wrap the meat in is lack luster. There is far too much filling for the three small tortillas. It was fairly good by flavor, but also room temp when it arrived. This was no fault of the DoorDash delivery person as they arrived within 30 minutes of placing my order.

Was passing through the area on I-94 and thought that Mexican food sounds good to me.  The restaurant is conveniently located near the interchange.

I stopped for a late lunch at los Tres Amigos Jackson.

It was a great experience. I had the fajitas and a margarita.

I was very lucky to have an excellent server, Ricardo.

Not only was the food fresh and very good, but Ricardo was extremely professional and very friendly. You can tell he really loves his job and cares about the customers.

I even asked him what his schedule was to make sure the next time I pass through here that I stop if he is working. He is off Wednesdays and Sundays. Make sure that you plan your trip around that!

That said, the food was good and fresh, reasonably priced, plentiful and the restaurant is very clean and inviting.

A good place, indeed.

review_text:
My husband and I have been coming here for over 10 years.   We always used to get top notch service, but things have changed.   This is the fifth time we have come here and received poor service.   One time we were seated at a table and no one came over.  We sat there for 15 minutes, the place was not busy.  Staff walked by us numerous times, but no one acknowledged we were there, so we left.  And the last four times, someone took our order, we received our food quickly, but then we never saw our waitress again.  We see them just sitting in the back booth by the kitchen talking and laughing.  While I appreciate it is hard to take breaks in the service industry, waiting over 20 minutes for your waitress to return for a refill is not a good business strategy.  This time, my husband had to walk back to the area they were all sitting, and he waved to our waitress, which got her to come to our table finally.   At this point we just asked for the bill so we could leave.

The food is always amazing, it is what has kept us coming back.  But not anymore.

review_text: If you like authentic fajitas where they skirt the beef and marinate the beef and chicken, then grill it right before putting it on a red hot iron and bring it out sizzling to your table, don't go here for that.  I've gotten good meals here before but the fajitas are probably the worst thing they do here and getting 3 tacos for $11 less would be a much better value.  Very little protein and if you were blindfolded you wouldn't be able to tell the difference between the chicken and the steak.  Both tough, dry and overcooked.  The corn tortillas looked like they were heated at lunch ( it was 6 pm). The mostly green pepper and onions were completely broken down and mushy.  They cook them ahead of time and hold them on a steamtable. There was maybe 2 tablespoons of pico de gallo that came with it and it was old and sour and had no cilantro or jalapenos.  The menu said choice of black bean refried or pinto beans but the pinto beans apparently are refried too.  The rice was cold.  An extremely loud blender or vacuum ran for 20 minutes in the kitchen.    I know this sounds petty but it was just awful food and for the same money I could have gotten their porterhouse.  I was greeted right away and the food only took minutes  (precooked) and the server checked back.  I'll go back, but I'll never order fajitas again

I placed a to go order tonight at 8:30 pm. The restaurant texted me and said my order would be ready at 9:03 pm. I sent my adult nephew in at 9pm to get the order. 20 minutes later, they still hadn't brought out the food. The guy manning the bar and the to-go orders was so busy flirting with these 2 chicks that he ignored us for 20 minutes. I even had to call out to him and wave to get him to even acknowledge my presence. When he finally came over, without the food, he gave the excuse that they were busy. I'm sorry but aside from the 2 chicks at the bar, there was only 1 other couple there. They were NOT busy. Then, they finally brought out my food and I told them I would not be back because of the crappy service. A different waiter then said, as I was leaving, "see you tomorrow" and then started laughing!
Completely unprofessional!  Don't bother coming here. There are other Mexican restaurants in Jackson that have better food and will treat you much better!

review_text:
We have eaten there many times over the past 3-4 years. This was for Father's Day and a birthday with 13 people. I called ahead to ensure they could accommodate one table w 13 people, in the side room. Was assured they could. They cannot.

We spilled salsa on the table shortly after being delivered. We apologized and the waiter said he'd take care of it. He did NOT till asked a 3rd time and only after we had finished our meals.

One entree was not what was ordered. Requested a menu to ensure the correct item was ordered; never provided. When the re-order came through, it did not contain the correct side dish. Asked 3 times, never corrected.

Another entree required corn tortilla (gluten allergy). Asked 4 times, finally brought after most of the others had completed their meal and that entree had cooled off.

When all of this was summed up to the waiter at the end of the meal, we were told "my bad, boss".

We stated at the beginning of the meal that we were celebrating a birthday. Most of the guests had left by the time we sang happy birthday at the end of the meal. There was zero recognition of a birthday, which is not consistent with other times we've eaten/celebrated there.

We will go elsewhere.
